title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)

seo.h1: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)
h2Headings: What is Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)?
Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup
How to Make Mercimek Corbasi: Step-by-Step
Expert Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of steaming Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) garnished with fresh herbs and a drizzle of chili oil.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, comforting flavors of this traditional Turkish Red Lentil Soup, a staple in Turkish cuisine.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish staple, a hearty and comforting red lentil soup known for its vibrant color and smooth texture. This traditional recipe combines simple ingredients to create a deeply flavorful dish, perfect for a warming meal any time of year. Enjoy the authentic taste of Turkey with every spoonful.

instructions.title: Prepare Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and carrot, sauteing for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Build the Base
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes, pressing it against the bottom of the pot to deepen its flavor. Sprinkle in the all-purpose flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to create a roux, ensuring no raw flour taste remains.

instructions.title: Add Lentils and Liquid
instructions.text: Add the rinsed and drained red lentils to the pot, stirring to coat them with the aromatic mixture. Pour in the hot vegetable broth or water, stirring well to combine and scraping up any bits from the bottom of the pot. Bring the mixture to a boil.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let the soup simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Blend to Smoothness
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. Alternatively, you can transfer batches to a stand blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot.

instructions.title: Season and Prepare Topping
instructions.text: Stir in salt and black pepper to taste. For the traditional topping, melt the butter in a small saucepan over medium-low heat. Once melted, add the dried mint and red pepper flakes, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant but not burnt. Be careful not to overheat the spices.

instructions.title: Serve Mercimek Corbasi
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into individual serving bowls. Drizzle each bowl generously with the spiced butter topping. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing, which brightens the soup's flavor beautifully.

tips: For an extra smooth soup, ensure your lentils are cooked until very soft before blending. Using an immersion blender directly in the pot saves on washing up and provides excellent results.
The spiced butter topping (nane yagli salça) is crucial for authentic flavor. Dont skip it! The dried mint and red pepper flakes add a wonderful aroma and gentle heat.

Mercimek Corbasi stores well. Leftovers can be refrigerated for up to 3-4 days or frozen for up to 3 months. Thaw and reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of water or broth if it has thickened too much.
article:
## Mercimek Corbasi: The Soul-Warming Turkish Red Lentil Soup

In the vast and rich tapestry of Turkish cuisine, few dishes evoke the same sense of comfort, warmth, and home as Mercimek Corbasi. This vibrant, creamy **Turkish Red Lentil Soup** is more than just a starter; it’s a culinary embrace, a staple found on every dinner table, from bustling city restaurants to cozy village kitchens. Simple in its ingredients yet profound in its flavor, Mercimek Corbasi is a testament to the power of humble ingredients transformed into something truly extraordinary. Whether you’re seeking a nutritious meal, a soothing remedy for a cold day, or simply a taste of authentic Turkey, this beloved soup is an absolute must-try. Its appealing reddish-orange hue, silky texture, and delicate balance of spices make it an instant favorite for all ages.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)?

Mercimek Corbasi translates directly to "lentil soup," but the red variety (kırmızı mercimek çorbası) is arguably the most famous and universally adored. Originating from Anatolia, where lentils have been a dietary cornerstone for millennia, this soup embodies the heartiness and resourcefulness of Turkish cooking. It's characterized by its smooth, velvety consistency, achieved by blending the cooked red lentils until no discernible pieces remain. The soup’s signature flavor profile comes from a harmonious blend of sautéed onions, garlic, tomato paste, and a crucial quartet of spices: dried mint, cumin, and paprika or red pepper flakes. Often finished with a drizzle of sizzling butter infused with more paprika and dried mint, it delivers a sensory experience that is both invigorating and deeply comforting. It’s a dish that speaks of tradition, hospitality, and the simple joys of a well-prepared meal.

## Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Creating an authentic **Turkish Red Lentil Soup** requires a handful of readily available ingredients, each playing a vital role in building its complex flavor profile.

*   **Red Lentils:** The star of the show. They cook quickly and break down easily, creating the soup's signature creamy texture. Ensure they are well-rinsed before cooking.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** The aromatic foundation. Sautéing them until soft and fragrant releases their sweet and pungent notes, building depth.
*   **Tomato Paste (Salça):** A key ingredient for color and umami. Turkish tomato paste is often more concentrated, so adjust quantity if using a different type.
*   **Olive Oil or Butter:** Used for sautéing the aromatics and sometimes for the final spiced butter drizzle. Butter adds a richer flavor.
*   **Dried Mint (Nane):** Absolutely essential. Its unique fresh, slightly peppery aroma is iconic in Turkish cuisine and pairs perfectly with lentils.
*   **Cumin:** Provides an earthy warmth that complements the lentils beautifully.
*   **Paprika (Tatlı Pul Biber) or Red Pepper Flakes (Acı Pul Biber):** For color and a gentle warmth. Use sweet paprika for a milder soup, or hot red pepper flakes for a subtle kick.
*   **Vegetable or Chicken Broth/Water:** The liquid base. Broth adds more flavor, but water works perfectly well, especially with the other robust ingredients.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To season and enhance all the flavors.
*   **Lemon Wedges:** Crucial for serving. A squeeze of fresh lemon brightens the entire soup.

## How to Make Mercimek Corbasi: Step-by-Step

Making this delicious soup is surprisingly straightforward. Follow these steps for a perfect bowl of Mercimek Corbasi:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Begin by thoroughly rinsing 1 cup of red lentils under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch, preventing the soup from becoming cloudy. Set aside.
2.  **Sauté the Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil or butter over medium heat. Add 1 finely chopped medium onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add 2 cloves of minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  **Build the Flavor Base:** Stir in 1-2 tablespoons of tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly. This step caramelizes the paste, deepening its flavor.
4.  **Add Spices:** Add 1 teaspoon of dried mint, 1 teaspoon of cumin, and 1 teaspoon of paprika (or red pepper flakes if you prefer heat). Stir for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
5.  **Combine Lentils and Liquid:** Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot, along with 6 cups of vegetable or chicken broth (or water). Bring the mixture to a boil.
6.  **Simmer:** Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely tender and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
7.  **Blend to Smoothness:** Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. Alternatively, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender (venting the lid for hot liquids) and blend until smooth.
8.  **Season and Adjust Consistency:** Return the blended soup to the stove over low heat. Season generously with salt and black pepper to taste. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot broth or water until it reaches your desired consistency. Simmer for a few more minutes to let the flavors meld.

## Expert Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Achieving that perfect, authentic Mercimek Corbasi involves a few key details:

*   **Rinse Those Lentils:** Don't skip rinsing! It's crucial for a clean-tasting, non-gummy soup.
*   **Sauté Properly:** Take your time with the onions and garlic. Their sweetness is the backbone of the soup. Cooking the tomato paste briefly also enhances its flavor.
*   **Don't Skimp on Dried Mint:** It’s a defining flavor of this **Turkish Red Lentil Soup**. Use good quality, fragrant dried mint.
*   **Blend Thoroughly:** The signature creamy texture comes from blending until absolutely no whole lentil pieces remain. An immersion blender makes this incredibly easy.
*   **The Lemon is Non-Negotiable:** A squeeze of fresh lemon juice at the end, either stirred in or added by each diner, brightens the soup and balances its richness. It transforms the flavor profile.
*   **The Spiced Butter Drizzle (Nane Yağı):** For an extra layer of flavor and a beautiful presentation, melt 1-2 tablespoons of butter in a small pan, add 1/2 teaspoon of paprika and 1/2 teaspoon of dried mint, and cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant. Drizzle this over individual bowls of soup just before serving. It adds richness and a burst of aromatic spice.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** Red lentils absorb a lot of liquid. Have extra hot broth or water on hand to thin the soup to your preferred consistency if it becomes too thick after blending.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Mercimek Corbasi is traditionally served hot, often as a starter, but it's substantial enough to be a light main course.

*   **Garnish:** Always serve with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing. A swirl of the spiced butter drizzle (nane yağı), a sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley or a few red pepper flakes (pul biber) are also common and delightful garnishes.
*   **Bread:** Crusty bread is a must for dipping. Think fresh Turkish bread (ekmek), pita, or even a good sourdough.
*   **Side Salad:** A simple shepherd's salad (çoban salatası) with finely diced tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, and parsley dressed with olive oil and lemon juice makes a refreshing accompaniment.
*   **Main Meals:** It perfectly precedes grilled meats like kebabs, chicken shish, or alongside rice pilafs.
*   **Beverages:** Turkish black tea or a refreshing glass of Ayran (a savory yogurt drink) are traditional pairings.

## Variations and Dietary Adaptations

Mercimek Corbasi is wonderfully adaptable to various dietary needs and preferences:

*   **Vegetarian & Vegan:** This soup is naturally vegan if you use vegetable broth and olive oil instead of butter. It's a fantastic plant-based protein source.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Red lentils are naturally gluten-free, making this soup an excellent option for those with gluten sensitivities. Just ensure your broth is also gluten-free.
*   **Spicier Version:** If you love heat, increase the amount of red pepper flakes (pul biber) in the initial spice blend or add a pinch of cayenne pepper.
*   **Heartier Soup:** For a more substantial meal, you can add finely diced carrots, potatoes, or celery along with the onions and garlic. They will soften and blend seamlessly into the soup.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A pinch of smoked paprika can add a subtle smoky depth, though it's not traditional.
*   **Herbal Additions:** While dried mint is classic, a sprinkle of fresh dill or parsley as a garnish can add another layer of freshness.

Mercimek Corbasi isn't just a recipe; it's an experience. It's a taste of Turkish hospitality, a bowl of warmth that nourishes both body and soul. Its simplicity belies a depth of flavor that will leave you craving more. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to fall in love with the comforting embrace of this iconic **Turkish Red Lentil Soup**. It’s a dish that truly brings people together and warms the heart.
